summary: Large item decompilation progress PR touching 22 files, primarily item C sources and item headers. The author explicitly says itlugia and itclimbersice are matching but their sdata2 order was not resolved. Bot report records +0.45% matched code, +17508 bytes, 69 new matches, and 11 unmatched-item improvements. Diff excerpt shows extensive symbols.txt metadata changes, including object size changes and many global data/string/float symbols converted to local @ labels.

what_changed: Touched 22 files: 14 item C sources/headers, 2 item aggregate headers, symbols.txt, configure.py, and mpcoll.c/mpcoll.h.
Advanced item decompilation matching across itzgshell, itclimbersstring, itlugia, itclimbersice, itoldottosea, itklap, itsscopebeam, itcrazyhandbomb, itseakchain, and it_2E5A.
Bot report recorded 69 new matches and 11 improvements in unmatched items for GALE01, including +17508 matched-code bytes.
Author specifically called out itlugia and itclimbersice as matching while noting unresolved sdata2 ordering.
Updated symbols.txt metadata, including data object sizes, local labels, local string labels, and many sdata/sdata2 float/double symbols changed from global address-style names to local @ labels.
Updated configure.py item object matching classifications; the excerpt shows itsscopebeam in the changed matching configuration area.
Updated item headers, including itCharItems.h, itCommonItems.h, and per-item headers for climbersstring, seakchain, lugia, klap, zgshell, and zrshell.

smart_moves.text: The PR separates a known data-order caveat from the text-match claim: itlugia and itclimbersice are described as matching, but sdata2 ordering is explicitly called unresolved.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md

smart_moves.text: The PR pairs source progress with symbol metadata updates rather than only adding C code; the diff adjusts sizes, scopes, and local labels for rodata/data/sdata/sdata2.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: symbols.txt hunks
changed_files.jsonl: symbols.txt +73/-68

smart_moves.text: Header surfaces were updated alongside item source progress, consistent with recovered signatures or shared item declarations being moved into item headers.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: itCharItems.h, itCommonItems.h, climbersstring.h, seakchain.h, lugia.h, klap.h, zgshell.h, zrshell.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item matching work, text-section matches may coexist with unresolved small-data ordering; record the caveat rather than overstating data completion.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: author notes itlugia and itclimbersice are matching but sdata2 order could not be corrected
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists new 100% matches for itlugia and itclimbersice

decomp_lessons.lesson: Large item progress can require synchronized edits across C sources, item headers, configure matching status, and symbols metadata.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: 22 changed files across item sources, headers, configure.py, and symbols.txt
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report records new matches and unmatched improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: Symbol metadata cleanup may involve converting address-style global data names into local @ labels when the diff evidence says those objects are local strings, floats, doubles, or labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: it_804DCC10..it_804DCC1C global sdata2 floats converted to @161/@198/@222/@223 local labels
raw_diff excerpt: it_803F8990 and it_803F89B8 global data strings converted to @288 and @367 local strings

naming_conventions.convention: The PR retains many unknown item functions under address-style it_802... names when semantics are not shown by the evidence.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report new matches and improvements list address-style item function names

naming_conventions.convention: Known item callbacks use item-prefixed motion/logic names such as itKlap_UnkMotion1_Coll, itSscopebeam_UnkMotion9_Coll, itCrazyhandbomb_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itOldottosea_UnkMotion2_Anim.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report new matches table

naming_conventions.convention: Local compiler/data labels in symbols.txt appear as @number entries, while some global address-style it_80... data symbols remain where not changed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: jumptable_803F6608 changed to @188; multiple sdata2 globals changed to @ labels; nearby it_804DCC20 remains global

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use bot/checkdiff-style progress reports to distinguish new full matches from partial unmatched improvements.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report separates 69 new matches from 11 improvements in unmatched items

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: When source functions match but sdata2 ordering remains off, keep the ordering issue explicit and avoid treating data order as solved.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust symbols metadata for exact section ownership and locality, including object size and local labels, when matching item data and literals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: it_803B86E8 size adjusted; many .data/.sdata/.sdata2 entries converted from global it_... names to local @ labels

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Promote item objects in configure.py only as matching evidence supports object-level status changes.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: configure.py +7/-7 in 5 hunks
raw_diff excerpt: configure.py changed item object classification near itsscopebeam
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report includes item units with new full matches

notes: Loaded diff is truncated, so detailed per-function source tactics inside each C file are not fully visible from the slice.
No human review comments are present; review feedback is limited to the author note and bot report.
The bot report supports many match/improvement claims, but the intake did not run builds or objdiff and does not independently validate them.
configure.py is known changed, but only a partial hunk is visible; exact object status changes beyond the shown itsscopebeam context are not fully enumerated.
Header changes are known by file metadata, but exact recovered prototypes/signatures are not visible in the loaded excerpt.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Separate text match from unresolved sdata2 ordering
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2421 is evidence that item functions can be reported as matching while sdata2 ordering remains unresolved; postmortems should preserve that caveat instead of converting it into a full data-match claim.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report new matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Item matching often spans source, headers, configure, and symbols metadata
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR's item matching progress changed item sources, item headers, configure.py, and symbols.txt; future item work should search all of those surfaces when a match depends on declarations, object status, or data/symbol ownership.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl
raw_diff excerpt
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report

source_update_candidates.title: Item matching surface includes aggregate headers and symbols metadata
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2421 item matching work touched src/melee/it/items/*.c, per-item headers, src/melee/it/itCharItems.h, src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h, configure.py, and config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-scope fact for future item decomp searches; should be curated before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Record unresolved small-data order separately from text match
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR author reports functions as matching but identifies unresolved sdata/sdata2 ordering, preserve the caveat and avoid claiming complete data ownership/order resolution.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: itlugia and itclimbersice matching with unresolved sdata2 order
source_update_candidates.reason: Consistent with existing verification/data-section standards, but PR evidence is one historical example rather than a full global rule by itself.

source_update_candidates.title: symbols.txt local @ labels used for item data/string/float symbols
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2421 changes many config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries in item-related regions from global address-style it_80... data symbols to local @number labels for .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 entries.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: it_803F8990 to @288, it_804DCC10 to @161, it_804DD678 to @172, and related hunks
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future symbol metadata searches, but exact ownership/locality should remain source-owned and not inferred beyond the shown diff.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every changed item source became fully matching; the bot report distinguishes 69 new matches from 11 partial improvements.
Do not claim sdata2 order was fixed for itlugia or itclimbersice; the author says it was not.
Do not infer reviewer intent or acceptance rationale; no human review comments were included.
Do not generalize every global-to-local symbol change as a universal naming rule without checking section ownership and current symbols metadata.
